The problem says "각 변수는 참(True) 또는 거짓(False)" but context: variables represent guilt? It says "누가 유죄이고 무죄입니까?" So True means guilty? They ask "소희: 참/거짓" So likely True means guilty. We must satisfy all 50 constraints. Each constraint is "다음 중 최소 하나는 참이다" meaning at least one of listed statements is true. Each statement is of form X은(는) 무죄 or 유죄. So we need to interpret: For each variable X, we assign guilty (True) or innocent (False). Then each clause is a disjunction of literals: e.g., "진우은(는) 무죄" is literal (not guilty) i.e., ¬진우. "현수은(는) 유죄" is literal 진우? Actually "현수은(는) 유죄" is literal 현수. So each clause is a disjunction of some literals. We need at least one true literal per clause.

We have 9 variables, 50 clauses. We need find assignment satisfying all.

We can solve logically or brute force. 2^9 = 512 possibilities. Could manually reason but easier to think logically. But we can also try to deduce contradictions.

Let's denote variables: S (소희), J (지우), Jn (진우), H (현수), Y (영호), D (동욱), M (지민), Se (서현), Mi (민석). True = guilty.

We need to satisfy all clauses. Let's write them.

I'll label clauses.

1. (진우은 무죄) ∨ (현수은 유죄) ∨ (지우은 유죄)
   => ¬Jn ∨ H ∨ J

2. (진우은 유죄) ∨ (민석은 무죄) ∨ (지우은 무죄) ∨ (지민은 유죄)
   => Jn ∨ ¬Mi ∨ ¬J ∨ M

3. (민석은 무죄) ∨ (동욱은 유죄) ∨ (지우은 무죄) ∨ (영호은 무죄)
   => ¬Mi ∨ D ∨ ¬J ∨ ¬Y

4. (현수은 무죄) ∨ (지우은 무죄) ∨ (서현은 유죄) ∨ (지민은 유죄)
   => ¬H ∨ ¬J ∨ Se ∨ M

5. (서현은 무죄) ∨ (민석은 무죄) ∨ (동욱은 유죄)
   => ¬Se ∨ ¬Mi ∨ D

6. (서현은 무죄) ∨ (동욱은 무죄) ∨ (지우은 유죄) ∨ (진우은 무죄)
   => ¬Se ∨ ¬D ∨ J ∨ ¬Jn

7. (영호, 서현 또는 진우 중 최소 하나는 유죄이다)
   => Y ∨ Se ∨ Jn

8. (영호은 무죄) ∨ (현수은 무죄) ∨ (민석은 무죄) ∨ (소희은 유죄)
   => ¬Y ∨ ¬H ∨ ¬Mi ∨ S

9. (진우은 유죄) ∨ (현수은 유죄) ∨ (소희은 무죄) ∨ (동욱은 무죄)
   => Jn ∨ H ∨ ¬S ∨ ¬D

10. (지우은 유죄) ∨ (현수은 무죄) ∨ (진우은 유죄) ∨ (동욱은 무죄)

11. (진우은 무죄) ∨ (서현은 무죄) ∨ (현수은 유죄) ∨ (민석은 무죄)

16. (동욱은 유죄) ∨ (민석은 유죄) ∨ (지우은 유죄) ∨ (서현은 무죄)
    => D ∨ Mi ∨ J ∨ ¬Se

17. (민석은 유죄) ∨ (소희은 무죄) ∨ (영호은 유죄)
    => Mi ∨ ¬S ∨ Y

    => ¬Y ∨ ¬M ∨ ¬H

25. (서현은 유죄) ∨ (소희은 무죄) ∨ (동욱은 무죄) ∨ (진우은 유죄)
    => Se ∨ ¬S ∨ ¬D ∨ Jn

    => Se ∨ S ∨ D

30. (지민은 무죄) ∨ (서현은 유죄) ∨ (현수은 유죄)
    => ¬M ∨ Se ∨ H

32. (지우은 무죄) ∨ (진우은 유죄) ∨ (동욱은 유죄)

33. (지민은 무죄) ∨ (서현은 유죄) ∨ (현수은 무죄) ∨ (동욱은 무죄)

35. (민석은 유죄) ∨ (지우은 무죄) ∨ (지민은 유죄)

analysisWe need to solve a logic puzzle with many constraints. We need assignment of 9 variables (I, A, H, K, G, M, J, B, L). Each either True (assigned) or False (unassigned). Constraints are many "at least one true" among given statements. Each statement is of form: X is assigned or X is unassigned. So each clause is disjunction of literals. We need find a satisfying assignment.

This is a SAT problem with 9 variables and 50 clauses. We can try reasoning manually but could be heavy. Let's attempt to deduce logically.

Let's denote variable truth as assigned = True. Unassigned = False.

We have many clauses. Let's list them with indices and literals.

I'll denote T for assigned, F for unassigned. Literal "X is assigned" = X. "X is unassigned" = ¬X.

Clauses:

1. K unassigned OR I assigned OR J assigned OR M assigned. => ¬K ∨ I ∨ J ∨ M

2. K unassigned OR H assigned OR J unassigned. => ¬K ∨ H ∨ ¬J

3. J assigned OR L unassigned OR G unassigned OR M assigned. => J ∨ ¬L ∨ ¬G ∨ M

4. L unassigned OR H unassigned OR I assigned OR G unassigned. => ¬L ∨ ¬H ∨ I ∨ ¬G

5. I unassigned OR H assigned OR J unassigned OR G assigned. => ¬I ∨ H ∨ ¬J ∨ G

6. J assigned OR A assigned OR K unassigned. => J ∨ A ∨ ¬K

7. H assigned OR G unassigned OR I assigned. => H ∨ ¬G ∨ I

8. M assigned OR A unassigned OR I assigned. => M ∨ ¬A ∨ I

9. B unassigned OR J unassigned OR H unassigned. => ¬B ∨ ¬J ∨ ¬H

10. A assigned OR J assigned OR G unassigned OR I assigned. => A ∨ J ∨ ¬G ∨ I

11. K unassigned OR M assigned OR H assigned OR J assigned. => ¬K ∨ M ∨ H ∨ J

12. B assigned OR J unassigned OR H assigned OR A assigned. => B ∨ ¬J ∨ H ∨ A

13. K assigned OR A unassigned OR H assigned. => K ∨ ¬A ∨ H

14. H unassigned OR B assigned OR G unassigned OR K unassigned. => ¬H ∨ B ∨ ¬G ∨ ¬K

15. A unassigned OR J unassigned OR M unassigned OR I unassigned. => ¬A ∨ ¬J ∨ ¬M ∨ ¬I

16. L unassigned OR I unassigned OR G unassigned. => ¬L ∨ ¬I ∨ ¬G

17. B unassigned OR I assigned OR A unassigned. => ¬B ∨ I ∨ ¬A

18. K assigned OR H unassigned OR M assigned OR J assigned. => K ∨ ¬H ∨ M ∨ J

19. I assigned OR J unassigned OR L unassigned. => I ∨ ¬J ∨ ¬L

20. I unassigned OR G unassigned OR L assigned. => ¬I ∨ ¬G ∨ L

21. I assigned OR B unassigned OR H unassigned OR L assigned. => I ∨ ¬B ∨ ¬H ∨ L

22. M assigned OR K assigned OR A assigned OR I unassigned. => M ∨ K ∨ A ∨ ¬I

23. M unassigned OR G unassigned OR A unassigned OR H unassigned. => ¬M ∨ ¬G ∨ ¬A ∨ ¬H

24. I unassigned OR L assigned OR J unassigned OR M assigned. => ¬I ∨ L ∨ ¬J ∨ M

25. M unassigned OR J assigned OR A assigned. => ¬M ∨ J ∨ A

26. J unassigned OR M unassigned OR A unassigned OR H unassigned. => ¬J ∨ ¬M ∨ ¬A ∨ ¬H

27. G assigned OR L unassigned OR I assigned. => G ∨ ¬L ∨ I

28. B unassigned OR I unassigned OR A assigned OR J unassigned. => ¬B ∨ ¬I ∨ A ∨ ¬J

29. L OR A OR I assigned. => L ∨ A ∨ I

30. A unassigned OR L unassigned OR J unassigned OR I unassigned. => ¬A ∨ ¬L ∨ ¬J ∨ ¬I

    => (S ∨ D ∨ ¬J ∨ Y)

    => (J ∨ ¬D ∨ E)

    => (E ∨ ¬S ∨ ¬Y)

We need a satisfying assignment.

We can attempt to use logical deduction: look for clauses that force something.

Clause 48: (¬T ∨ ¬D ∨ ¬J ∨ ¬E). That means at least one of T,D,J,E is absent. So not all four can be true. So at least one is false.

Clause 14: (H ∨ D ∨ E ∨ M). So at least one of H,D,E,M attends.

Clause 18: (M ∨ T ∨ J ∨ E). At least one of M,T,J,E attends.

Clause 35: (S ∨ ¬H ∨ ¬D). So either S attends, or H absent, or D absent.

Clause 37: (¬S ∨ ¬D ∨ R). So if S attends and D attends then R must attend. Actually clause says at least one of ¬S, ¬D, R is true. So if S and D are true, then R must be true. Good.

Clause 45: (¬M ∨ ¬R ∨ ¬D). So not all M,R,D can be true simultaneously. At least one absent among M,R,D.

Clause 25: (R ∨ ¬D ∨ ¬M). So if D and M are true, then R must be true. (same as 37 but different). Good.

Clause 21: (J ∨ ¬D ∨ ¬M). So if D and M true, then J true.

Let's denote variables: T, N, S, B, St, Nu, R, F, C. (T=타코, N=국, S=샐러드, B=버거, St=스테이크, Nu=국수, R=밥, F=생선, C=카레)

We need all clauses true.

Clause 52: at least one of 국, 스테이크, 버거 true. So N or St or B true.

Clause 1: at least one of S true or B false or T true or C true. Equivalent: S ∨ ¬B ∨ T ∨ C.

Clause 2: at least one of Nu false or F false or B false. So ¬Nu ∨ ¬F ∨ ¬B.

Clause 3: at least one of St false or Nu true or C false. So ¬St ∨ Nu ∨ ¬C.

Clause 4: S ∨ ¬B ∨ N ∨ Nu.

Clause 5: ¬F ∨ R ∨ ¬N ∨ Nu.

Clause 38: T ∨ ¬N ∨ R.

Clause 41: ¬F ∨ ¬C ∨ St ∨ ¬R.

Clause 42: C ∨ ¬St ∨ ¬R.

Clause 43: ¬R ∨ ¬S ∨ ¬B.

Clause 45: F ∨ ¬B ∨ ¬T ∨ Nu.

Clause 46: B ∨ ¬R ∨ Nu ∨ ¬C.

Clause 47: ¬C ∨ ¬F ∨ ¬N ∨ ¬B.

Clause 48: R ∨ ¬B ∨ ¬S.

Clause 49: ¬S ∨ N ∨ T ∨ C.

Clause 50: ¬N ∨ B ∨ St ∨ ¬S.

Clause 51: ¬C ∨ St ∨ B ∨ Nu.

Clause 52 already.

We need find assignment.

Let's attempt use deduction.

From clause 2: at least one of ¬Nu, ¬F, ¬B. So cannot have all Nu, F, B true simultaneously. So at least one of them false.

Clause 3: ¬St ∨ Nu ∨ ¬C. So if St true and C true then Nu must be true. If St true and C false then Nu could be false? Wait clause: ¬St ∨ Nu ∨ ¬C. If St true and C true then need Nu true. If St true and C false, then clause satisfied by ¬C true? Actually C false gives ¬C true, so satisfied. So only problematic when St true and C true and Nu false. So we must avoid that combination.

Clause 4: S ∨ ¬B ∨ N ∨ Nu. So if B true, then need at least one of S,N,Nu true.

Clause 5: ¬F ∨ R ∨ ¬N ∨ Nu. So if F true and N true then need R true or Nu true.

Clause 6: St ∨ ¬B ∨ S ∨ ¬Nu. So if B true and Nu true then need St or S true.

Clause 7: ¬F ∨ St ∨ ¬N. So if F true and N true then need St true.

Clause 8: ¬F ∨ ¬R ∨ ¬T ∨ ¬Nu. So if F true and R true and T true and Nu true then clause false. So cannot have all those four true simultaneously.

Clause 9: R ∨ N ∨ ¬T ∨ Nu. So if T true and Nu false then need R or N true.

Clause 10: R ∨ N ∨ ¬St. So if St true then need R or N true.

Clause 11: ¬C ∨ S ∨ T ∨ N. So if C true and S false and T false and N false then clause false. So at least one of those true.

Clause 12: ¬F ∨ ¬T ∨ Nu. So if F true and T true then need Nu true.

Clause 13: C ∨ ¬N ∨ St. So if N true then need C or St true.

Clause 14: F ∨ R ∨ ¬Nu. So if Nu true then need F or R true.

Clause 15: N ∨ S ∨ F ∨ ¬Nu. So if Nu true then need N or S or F true.

Clause 16: St ∨ ¬F ∨ ¬Nu ∨ ¬R. So if F true and Nu true and R true then need St true.

Clause 17: S ∨ ¬F ∨ ¬B ∨ C. So if F true and B true and C false then need S true.

Clause 18: ¬St ∨ S ∨ R ∨ ¬F. So if St true and F true then need S or R true.

Clause 19: Nu ∨ ¬St ∨ ¬N. So if St true and N true then need Nu true.

Clause 20: ¬N ∨ R ∨ ¬C. So if N true and C true then need R true.

Clause 21: B ∨ ¬N ∨ C ∨ S. So if N true then need B or C or S true.

Clause 22: F ∨ ¬N ∨ C. So if N true then need F or C true.

Clause 23: ¬S ∨ St ∨ ¬Nu. So if S true and Nu true then need St true.

Clause 25: T ∨ ¬C ∨ ¬B. So if B true and C true then need T true.

Clause 26: T ∨ ¬St ∨ C. So if St true and C false then need T true.

Clause 27: B ∨ ¬St ∨ N. So if St true and N false then need B true.

Clause 28: ¬F ∨ Nu ∨ N ∨ R. So if F true then need Nu or N or R true.

Clause 29: F
